ip_outcome_note: Species name printed on card face; PokemonTCG.io API (swsh3-25) confirms name=Heatran, nationalPokedexNumbers=[485], Basic, rarity=Rare Holo. Depicted massive quadrupedal metallic creature with cross-shaped facial markings in volcanic environment matches canonical Heatran appearance exactly.

trivia_bullets.text: Heatran is National Pokedex no. 485, the only Fire/Steel-type Pokemon in the franchise, and a Legendary introduced in Generation IV (Pokemon Diamond and Pearl, 2006). It is also the only Legendary Pokemon with a 50/50 gender ratio — it can be male or female. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-25; Bulbapedia: Heatran, via WebSearch snippet]

In the Sinnoh games, Heatran is a post-game encounter at Stark Mountain — accessible only after defeating the Pokemon League. The encounter is tied to Buck (an NPC who temporarily accompanies the player): Buck removes the Magma Stone from Stark Mountain's deepest chamber, which disturbs Heatran and triggers volcanic unrest. After Buck returns the stone, Heatran appears as a catchable level 70 Legendary in that chamber. [Bulbapedia: Stark Mountain, via WebSearch snippet; WebSearch: Heatran Stark Mountain Sinnoh post-game]
Flavor text: "Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves." — printed on DAA-025. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-25]

Heatran's Steel typing is physically expressed in its design: its body is described as iron-like metal partially melted by its own internal heat, and it has cross-shaped claws capable of digging into walls and ceilings. The cross-shaped face marking in the card art is a direct canonical visual identifier — the same cross motif appears across all official Heatran depictions. Art on DAA-025 is by Anesaki Dynamic, a studio/artist known for dramatic, high-contrast legendary Pokemon compositions. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh3-25 artist field; Bulbapedia: Heatran design notes, via WebSearch snippet]
